summary: A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000001) of type Type-A has been filed regarding a transaction between customer John Jensen and counterparty Jeremy Martinez. The transaction occurred on 2022-10-07, involving a cash deposit transfer of NPR 535368.64. The transaction involved account numbers NP00000000003412850188 and NP00000000003980427782 at banks PCBL and SBL, respectively. The countries involved include GB. The reason for the suspicious transaction observation is not further detailed in the available narrative. All relevant information, including customer, counterparty, banks, amount, date, transaction mode, and account numbers, has been included in this summary as per the available structured facts and narrative.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000002) was filed for Sally Dominguez, a customer of ADBL, due to a transaction that triggered an internal threshold alert. On 2022-10-07, Dominguez conducted a cash withdrawal transfer of NPR 21,807.13 to Henry King, with account numbers NP00000000000983174071 and NP00000000000894070684 involved. The transaction was routed through HBL and involved countries GB and IN. The amount and velocity of the transaction were deemed inconsistent with Dominguez's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis, noting that no adverse media or sanctions matches were found, and the customer has been with the institution for several years. The transaction mode was a cash withdrawal transfer, and the report type is Type-B.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000003) was filed for customer Cook, Flores and Ray due to a transaction that triggered an internal threshold alert. On 2022-10-07, the customer conducted a cross-border transfer of NPR 1,214,019.81 to counterparty Chad Mccarty, utilizing accounts NP00000000002968635055 and NP00000000000310823462 at banks KUMARI and SBL. The transaction involved countries GB and MX. The amount and velocity of the transfer raised concerns as they appear inconsistent with the customer's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis due to the pattern of transfers, which were executed in rapid succession and kept below reporting thresholds, but no adverse media or sanctions matches were identified, and the customer has been with the institution for some years.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000004) was filed for Robert King's account at PRABHU, which also involved LAXMI bank, due to a transaction on 2022-10-07. The transaction was a cross-border transfer of NPR 813935.97 to Sarah English. The transfer involved account numbers NP00000000001187666276 and NP00000000004011650754, and occurred between GB and NG. The transaction triggered an internal threshold alert, prompting a review that noted the transaction's value and velocity were not fully consistent with Robert King's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis, citing a pattern potentially consistent with structuring, where transfers were executed in rapid succession, possibly to avoid reporting thresholds. No adverse media or sanctions matches were found, and the customer has been with the institution for several years.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000006) was filed for customer Robert Gould due to a transaction that triggered an internal threshold alert. A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000007) was filed for Kimberly Aguirre's account (NP00000000000156976521) at NIMB, which involved a cross-border transfer of NPR 10860.8 to Oconnor and Sons on 2022-10-07. Although no adverse media or sanctions matches were found, the pattern of transfers, which were executed in rapid succession and below reporting thresholds, raised concerns. The report is submitted for further analysis and does not imply wrongdoing. The customer has been with the institution for several years, which was considered during the review.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000008) was filed for Xavier Bush, a customer of CBL, due to transactions that raised concerns. Cooke, with transactions involving account numbers NP00000000001711706029 and NP00000000004022184699 at banks CBL and ADBL. The report noted unusual transaction timing and velocity inconsistent with Bush's declared income profile. The customer has been with the institution for some years, and no adverse media or sanctions matches were found. The transactions were domestic, although the counterparties, including C. Cooke, and possibly others like Jennifer Kaiser, were involved in transactions amounting to approximately NPR 3,002,367. The report was submitted for further analysis, involving countries including GB.

The customer's declared income profile is listed as modest in their KYC file, which appears inconsistent with the transaction velocity and aggregate value. No adverse media or sanctions matches were identified, and the customer has a long-standing relationship with the institution. The report was submitted for further analysis due to the pattern of transactions, which included multiple transfers below reporting thresholds executed in rapid succession.
